title: The Protection of Plant Varieties and Farmers Rights Act, 2001
full_text: The Protection of Plant Varieties and Farmers Rights Act, 2001 पौधा किस्‍म और कृषक अधिकार संरक्षणअधिनियम, 2001 Rules Regulations Notifications Orders Circulars Ordinance Statutes SectionsScheduleAnnexureAppendixFormsActdetails Act ID: 200153 Act Number: 53 Enactment Date: 2001-10-30 Act Year: 2001 Short Title: The Protection of Plant Varieties and Farmers Rights Act, 2001 Hindi Title: पौधा किस्‍म और कृषक अधिकार संरक्षणअधिनियम, 2001 Long Title: An Act to provide for the establishment of an effective system for protection of plant varieties, the rights of farmers and plant breeders and to encourage the development of new varieties of plants. Ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare Department: Department of Agriculture, Cooperation and Farmers Welfare Enforcement Date: 11-11-2005 Notification: 11th November, 2005, ss. 2 to 13 (both inclusive) and ss. 95, 96 and 97, vide notification No. S.O. 1588(E), dated 11th November, 2005, see Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, sec. 3(ii). 19th October, 2006, s. 1 and ss. 14 to 94 (both inclusive) vide notification No. S.O. 1797(E), dated 19th October, 2006, see Gazette of India Extraordinary, Part II, sec. 3(ii). TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles Show all section The Protection of Plant Varieties and Farmers Rights Act, 2001 CHAPTER I PRELIMINARY CHAPTER II PROTECTION OF PLANT VARIETIES AND FARMERS’ RIGHTS AUTHORITY AND REGISTRY Protection of Plant Varieties and Farmers’ Rights Authority Registry CHAPTER III REGISTRATION OF PLANT VARIETIES AND ESSENTIALLY DERIVED VARIETY Application for registration Registration of essentially derived variety CHAPTER IV DURATION AND EFFECT OF REGISTRATION AND BENEFIT SHARING CHAPTER V SURRENDER AND REVOCATION OF CERTIFICATE AND RECTIFICATION AND CORRECTION OF REGISTER CHATER VI FARMERS’ RIGHTS CHAPTER VII COMPULSORY LICENCE CHAPTER VIII APPEALS CHAPTER IX FINANCE, ACCOUNTS AND AUDIT CHAPTER X INFRINGEMENT, OFFENCES, PENALTIES AND PRODURE Infringement Offences, penalties and procedure CHAPTER XI MISCELLANEOUS Section 1. Registrable varieties. Section 16. Persons who may make application. Section 17. Compulsory variety denomination. Section 18. Form of application. Show Related Subordinates Section 19. Test to be conducted. Show Related Subordinates Section 20. Acceptance of application or amendment thereof. Section 21. Advertisement of application. Show Related Subordinates Section 22. Registrar to consider grounds of opposition. Section 23. Registration of essentially derived variety. Show Related Subordinates Section 24. Issue of certificate of registration. Show Related Subordinates Section 25. Publication of list of varieties. Section 26. Determination of benefit sharing by Authority. Show Related Subordinates Section 27. Breeder to deposit seeds or propagating material. Show Related Subordinates Section 28. Registration to confer right. Show Related Subordinates Section 29. Exclusion of certain varieties. Show Related Subordinates Section 30. Researcher’s rights. Section 31. Special provisions relating to application for registration from citizens of convention countries. Section 32. Provisions as to reciprocity. Section 33. Surrender of certificate of registration. Show Related Subordinates Section 34. Revocation of protection on certain grounds. Show Related Subordinates Section 35. Payment of annual fees and forfeiture of registration in default thereof. Show Related Subordinates Section 36. Power to cancel or change registration and to rectify the Register. Show Related Subordinates Section 37. Correction of Register. Show Related Subordinates Section 38. Alteration of denomination of a registered variety. Show Related Subordinates Section 39. Farmers’ rights. Show Related Subordinates Section 40. Certain information to be given in application for registration. Section 41. Rights of communities. Show Related Subordinates Section 42. Protection of innocent infringement. Section 43. Authorisation of farmers’ variety. Section 44. Exemption from fees. Section 45. Gene Fund. Show Related Subordinates Section 46. Framing of schemes, etc. Section 47. Power of Authority to make order for compulsory licence in certain circumstances. Show Related Subordinates Section 48. When requirement of public deemed to have not been satisfied. Section 49. Adjournment of application for grant of compulsory licence. Section 50. Duration of compulsory licence. Show Related Subordinates Section 51. Authority to settle terms and conditions of licence. Section 52. Revocation of compulsory licence. Show Related Subordinates Section 53. Modification of compulsory licence. Section 54. Tribunal. Section 55. Composition of Tribunal. Section 56. Appeals to High Court. Show Related Subordinates Section 57. Orders of High Court. Section 58. Procedure of Tribunal. Section 59. Transitional provision. Section 60. Grants by Central Government. Section 61. Authority Fund. Section 62. Budget, accounts and audit. Show Related Subordinates Section 63. Financial and administrative powers of Chairperson. Show Related Subordinates Section 64. Infringement. Section 65. Suit for infringement, etc. Section 66. Relief in suits for infringement. Section 67. Opinion of scientific adviser. Section 68. Prohibition to apply denomination of a registered variety. Show Related Subordinates Section 69. Meaning of falsely applying denomination of a registered variety. Section 70. Penalty for applying false denomination, etc. Section 71. Penalty for selling varieties to which false denomination is applied, etc. Section 72. Penalty for falsely representing a variety as registered. Section 73. Penalty for subsequent offence. Section 74. No offence in certain cases. Section 75. Exemption of certain persons employed in ordinary course of business. Section 76. Procedure where invalidity of registration is pleaded by the accused. Section 77. Offences by companies. Section 78. Protection of security of India. Section 79. Implied warranty on sale of registered variety, etc. Section 80. Death of party to a proceeding. Section 81. Right of registered agent and the registered licensee to institute suit. Show Related Subordinates Section 82. Evidence of entry in Register, etc., and things done by the Authority and the Registrar. Section 83. Authority, Registrar and other officers not compellable to production of Register, etc. Show Related Subordinates Section 84. Document open to public inspection. Show Related Subordinates Section 85. Report of Authority to be placed before Parliament. Section 86. Government to be bound. Section 87. Proceedings before Authority or Registrar. Section 88. Protection of action taken in good faith. Section 89. Bar of jurisdiction. Section 90. Members and staff of Authority, etc., to be public servants. Section 91. Exemption from tax on wealth and income. Section 92. Act to have overriding effect. Section 93. Power of Central Government to give directions. Section 94. Power to remove difficulties. Section 95. Power to make regulations. Show Related Subordinates Section 96. Power of Central Government to make rules. Show Related Subordinates Section 97. Rules, regulations and schemes to be laid before Parliament.
